title: Checkbox Checked/Unchecked Automation Trigger and Conditional
excerpt:
Hi, it would be really great to have a simple boolean based trigger/conditional for checkbox driven automation. 
So, “If Checkbox changes to Checked/Unchecked then Do Something” or “If Something Changes and If Checkbox i…

posts.author: dan_ts (Dan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, it would be really great to have a simple boolean based trigger/conditional for checkbox driven automation.

So, “If Checkbox changes to Checked/Unchecked then Do Something” or “If Something Changes and If Checkbox is Checked/Unchecked then Do Something”

I know this can be done with Statuses but this type of simple base automation for several “Yes/No” or “True/False” type intuitive interactions on items will save time and money for all Monday.com users, especially with mobile app based workforces.

posts.author: carolinefox (Caroline F)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed-- I second this.

posts.author: SimonLogan (Simon Logan)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah I agree too, would be very useful.

posts.author: Catarina (Catarina Almeida)
posts.cookedHtml: Please let’s do that

posts.author: heyalec (Alec Gorecki)
posts.cookedHtml: My vote for this too please!

posts.author: nwoodsmith (Nathan)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be nice to have the option for an action to only occur on a specific check (uncheck) and not on the reverse action. This might make a good ‘and only if’ sub-option when choosing a check box column as part of a ‘when column changes’ automation.

posts.author: UBS (Jim Lyles)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a great feature. Especially if the checkbox column was allowed in forms so actions could be initiated when the only thing a customer needs to do is accept a condition, eliminating the need to have to choose a status drop down choice in the form.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @UBS - what we have done is utilized another column (status or number) column to see if the checkbox is checked/unchecked.

Lets use a status column named “Checkbox Status” with its default value called “Unchecked” and another value called “checked”. Then utilize the following stock automations:

When Checkbox changes and Checkbox Status is Unchecked, set Checkbox Status to Checked
When Checkbox changes and Checkbox Status is Checked, set Checkbox Status to Unchecked

This way you know the status of the checkbox in automations.

Hope this helps,
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: UBS (Jim Lyles)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Mark, Thanks for the reply! I’m fairly new with Monday and can’t find the stock automation that says ‘When Checkbox changes’ or any automation that has the checkbox column mentioned. I’ve used the search feature in the automations looking for a checkbox automation and scrolled through the list but can’t locate it. Any guidance will be greatly appreciated and thanks again.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @UBS - apologies I shouldn’t have said stock as they are custom automations.

Within the custom builder, start with “when column changes”

And add on an “and only if” status is something:

Then change status to something:

image522×563 26.7 KB

Once completed duplicate the automation and change it to the opposite values. This will give you the below two automations:

image1245×239 28.7 KB

Thanks!
Mark

posts.cookedHtml: Hi Mark, I see how to set that up that up now and just tested it. Works flawlessly! Genius! I bookmarked Green Llamas Consulting so I can reach out if I get stuck on things when building out my platform. Thanks again!

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)

posts.author: LtgPro (ASHLEY BOOHER)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Is there a way to have the check automated if the status changes to something or an item is created?

posts.author: SimonLogan (Simon Logan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Although adding another field to check the status of the checkbox field then to run the automation via that second field works, to me it’s insane that the checkbox field isn’t available to automations in the first place and that we have to add extra fields just to check its value.

I took a different approach which was to abandon use of the checkbox field altogether (if required for automations) and to use a STATUS field instead. I then created two possible statuses and used an ASCII X for the default (✗) and an ASCII check for the “checked” status (). So it looks like this on my board:

Google Chrome - Pinnacle Fitness 2023-06-09 at 10.05.27 AM2660×508 107 KB

The first column is the checkbox column (no longer needed) and the second is my status column using the ASCII characters to replicate the checkbox visually.

The only downside is that there is an extra click involved, one to trigger the status dropdown to appear then another to select, but this approach saves the need to create extra columns and automations as I can now use that STATUS directly in my automations or view filters.

posts.author: IanDirnfeld (IanDee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Simon hi
This is brilliant - can you share how to use ASCII codes in a Status column?
It would really help me.
Thanks,
Ian

posts.author: SimonLogan (Simon Logan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Ian

You can simply copy and paste the characters from that post I made or if you Google for “ascii character table” you’ll find sites which give you all available characters and you should be able to copy and paste from those.

posts.author: IanDirnfeld (IanDee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Simon,
Thanks for replying.
I am more curious how you used ASCII codes within the Status columns to achieve the visual checkmark.
Is there a special way to code it within the Status column?

posts.author: IanDirnfeld (IanDee)
posts.cookedHtml: No matter how I type it into the Status column, I get this:

posts.author: SimonLogan (Simon Logan)
posts.cookedHtml: Sorry if I wasn’t clear but I was copying and pasting the actual character itself, not the ASCII code for it? So literally just highlight the checkmark in my previous post then in your status column paste that in - let me know if that works as I’m sure that’s all I did!

posts.author: IanDirnfeld (IanDee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got it thanks.
Copying the X worked fine, but the check-mark wouldn’t copy so I just went to Word, and held down the Alt key and typed 10003 on the numeric keypad.
Worked like magic, and bottom line, no offense to Green Llamas, but this is a more robust solution - thanks!

posts.author: Lorelle (Lorelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
I love this, but it seems that it doesn’t work with subitems… yet. Have you found a solution for subitems?

I’m scrambling to automatically add a work doc to a column in a subitem at the moment it is created. I can do it with items but not subitems. I thought a checkbox or status change might work, but it doesn’t work with subitems, that I’ve found. Thanks.

posts.author: jjordan (Jake J)
posts.cookedHtml:
This tacks onto Green Llamas solution for check boxes doing automations. I wanted to run the process straight from a form, chain and the Monday help chat helped me do it. I needed the flow that if a a check box is check on the form, when the item is created, a specific subitem is made. It is for a simple check box to a client to use on a form, for example, “Do you want this page included in the document?” (Check is Yes, no check is no)

I needed to get the “General Caster” app from the app store.
For Every check column I made, I needed a corresponding single status column, and I used a Gen. Caster integration (Pic 1). My Columns are called Cover Page. Then I used the automation (Pic 2) to create a subitem to my specifications.

Pic 1

Gen Caster Integration - have a check column change a status1391×656 54.2 KB

Pic 2

Automation1260×148 21.2 KB

Shout out to Dieter @ monday.com support

posts.author: Lorelle (Lorelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
I third, fourth, and 85th this.

I have multiple checkboxes (true/false) options in a form. I need to add automation that will:

  1. Create a subitem based on if the individual checkbox option is true.
  2. Notify people that action is needed on this data if any of multiple checkboxes are true.

I’m playing with General Caster, but still cannot find a way to automate based upon formulas yet (The formula column sets the options based upon an OR statement covering multiple checkboxes).

True/False questions and options are SOP, so very strange that automation for these wouldn’t be a default trigger.

Thanks.

posts.author: BNBLEATHER (BNB)
posts.cookedHtml: Also looking for a similar solution, I try to avoid add on apps where possible, a tthe moment I am using lables with forms to then activate certain subitems elsewhere but its quite a work around for somehting that a check box should be able to do.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Shannon here with the monday team. Thank you all for your continued patience and feedback. We hear you and understand how valuable it would be to include the Checkbox Column within automation triggers and conditions.

That said, we’ve made some recent adjustments to the prioritization of our upcoming automation feature releases. For now, our focus is on a few other capabilities in that domain. While this particular feature isn’t in active development at the moment, it’s still on our radar for future exploration. We’ll be sure to keep you in the loop as things evolve, as always.

posts.author: dan_ts (Dan)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for responding on behalf of the Monday Team, Shannon. However, It’s been almost four and a half years since I started this thread, and my company left Monday.com due to incompatibilities like this, I’m afraid. We’ve got a great platform we’re on now though, and I’m happy to see your response and know that we made the right choice to leave since simple, basic things, like this request that can have a huge impact and return on efficiency, are not part of the Monday.com roadmap. Thank you
